title: White Container Text
description: Makes text in containers white in as many languages as I have energy/motivation for
body:
# White Container Text

This resource pack makes all text "written on" ***vanilla*** containers and the advancement screen white (instead of dark grey). This makes text with dark mode resource packs a lot more readable and nice-looking.

A lot of resource packs don't change this colour so I decided to make a standalone resource pack that you can add on top of **any** dark mode resource pack. The one problem with this being a resource pack is that I have to manually make the text white for each language separately. While I am definitely not going to add support for each and every one of them, I will try to add support for some of the more popular languages over time, though no promises at all.

White Container Text also fixes some minor language file inconsistencies (as of v0.2), such as fixing the inconsistency of capitalising all words except prepositions for boats with chests in `nl_be`, even though most other translation keys in said language file only have the first letter of the sentence capitalised, or removing unusual dots such as "." in "Herramientas de admin." in `es_es`. Do note that only containers have these fixes; If other translation keys have the same inconsistency but are not a part of the translation keys White Text Container modifies they will not be fixed by this pack.

> If you're using mods that add GUI elements or you're using a language that is not supported by White Container Text, I suggest using [Mindful Darkness](https://modrinth.com/mod/mindful-darkness) instead, if it has a version for the right modloader and Minecraft version for your scenario. If you do and you only want it to make text white, turn __Texture Darkness__ to __1.0__, turn __Font Brightness__ to __1.0__, and make sure __Dark Theme__ is __ON__ in the config. A subjective downside of this mod is that coloured text gets brightened too however (in newer versions of the mod at least).

## Supported Languages

As of v0.1, the following languages are supported (listed as shown in the language menu):

+ English (United Kingdom)
+ English (US)
+ Nederlands (Nederland)

As of v0.2, the following languages are supported (listed as shown in the language menu):

+ English (United Kingdom)
+ English (US)
+ Español (España)
+ Vlaams (België)
+ Nederlands (Nederland)

## Side Note

If you're wondering what GUI resource pack I'm using in the pictures shown in this project's gallery and as the icon, it is an unfinished version of (the now finished) [SmooGUI Dark](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/smoogui-dark).
